meta.title: 35th Bulletin of the Grande Armée (Austerlitz aftermath)

lexical_units.gloss_notes: French `mort` may be literal battlefield death or part of sacrificial/glory framing.

lexical_units.basic_meaning_source: Littre / Dictionnaire de l'Academie francaise, historical sense
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: monarchical or military memory is mediated through tomb, trophies, or crown objects
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political-military legitimacy may be understood through symbolic objects.
lexical_units.basic_meaning: burial monument, captured/displayed spoils, or physical crown
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The objects may be literal, but they also condense monarchy, victory, and imperial memory.
lexical_units.review_notes: Kept uncertain to avoid over-reading literal trophy/crown/tomb references. Gallica OCR noise is visible in the local sentence; use the French span_text as reviewed source text and verify before quotation.
lexical_units.gloss_notes: French symbolic-object terms need context-specific translation before CMT mapping.
